evidence.claim: Designed hybrid retrieval system (BM25 + dense vector recall) serving 50M+ queries/month
evidence.source_text: Designed the company's first hybrid retrieval system combining BM25 with dense vector recall, serving 50M+ queries per month.

evidence.claim: Built RAG-based ranking pipeline with BM25, BGE embeddings, FAISS HNSW, and LLM re-ranker
evidence.source_text: Built a RAG-based ranking pipeline... The architecture combined BM25 + dense retrieval (BGE embeddings, FAISS HNSW) with an LLM-based re-ranker

evidence.claim: Designed offline evaluation framework (NDCG, MRR, recall@K) calibrated against online A/B metrics
evidence.source_text: Designed the offline evaluation framework from scratch — NDCG, MRR, recall@K calibrated against online A/B engagement metrics.
Drove the migration over 4 months including the recruiter-feedback loop that surfaced reranking edge cases.

evidence.claim: Fine-tuned LLaMA-2-7B and Mistral-7B using LoRA and QLoRA
evidence.source_text: Fine-tuned LLaMA-2-7B and Mistral-7B variants using LoRA and QLoRA for domain-specific candidate-JD matching.

evidence.claim: Curated 200K high-quality preference pairs for model training
evidence.source_text: Built the data curation pipeline that generated 200K high-quality preference pairs from recruiter labels

evidence.claim: Deployed model via BentoML on Kubernetes with sub-200ms p95 latency
evidence.source_text: Deployed the model via BentoML on Kubernetes with sub-200ms p95 latency by quantizing to INT8 and batching at the request level.

evidence.claim: Reduced inference cost from $0.04 to under $0.001
evidence.source_text: Cost per inference dropped from $0.04 with GPT-3.5-fallback to under $0.001.

evidence.claim: Holds Google Cloud Professional ML Engineer certification
evidence.source_text: Google Cloud Professional ML Engineer (Google Cloud, 2019)

evidence.claim: Strong opinions on LLM vs. classical IR trade-offs
evidence.source_text: I have strong opinions about when LLMs are the right hammer and when classical IR is — usually it's both.
